title: AI band Bleeding Verse’s creator signs deal with Hallwood Media - Music Ally
text:
Last week
we wrote about AI band Bleeding Verse
, whose Spotify listeners had just overtaken one of their main influences, human band Holding Absence. Now there has been a development.
“Just wanted to give you guys an update and let you know that I signed a deal with Hallwood Media,” Bleeding Verse’s creator announced
in a post for their Patreon members
. The project currently has nearly 926,000 monthly listeners on Spotify, with its most popular track – ‘If You Loved Me Then’ – on nearly 2.9m streams so far.
Its creator joins a roster that
also includes Xania Monet
, an AI artist created by Suno user Telisha Jones, who currently has 912,000 Spotify listeners. Hallwood Media, a label launched by former Geffen exec Neil Jacobsen, signed another Suno user called ‘imoliver’ to a deal earlier this year.
He is currently running a pre-save campaign
for a new track called ‘Bury Me With My Phone’, which comes out this Friday.
The popularity of these GenAI projects is controversial: Holding Absence’s frontman certainly wasn’t impressed with the rise of Bleeding Verse, while the use of Suno – still being sued by major labels – also rubs rightsholders up the wrong way. However, Hallwood Media appears to be building momentum with each addition to its ranks.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
